codeReview.summary: The fix resolves the prior blocking gaps from extract-shared-idempotency-framework: the previously remaining local idempotency helpers in SalesReturnService and ProductionCatalogService are absent at the reviewed commit, and helper usage is routed through core.idempotency utilities.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature-level verification for idempotency deduplication is name-based (`private.*sha256Hex|normalizeToken|normalizeAmount`), which allows semantically similar local helpers to be retained under renamed methods instead of proving full semantic centralization.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json (fix-remaining-idempotency-duplicates.verificationSteps) uses grep on method names; handoff whatWasImplemented explicitly lists helper renames (e.g., normalizeAmount->roundedAmount/absoluteAmount, normalizeToken->normalizeUpperToken) to satisfy duplicate checks.

summary: Pass: compared with the original failing commit (a687a068), this fix state eliminates the blocking remnants (SalesReturnService local normalizeToken/sha256Hex and ProductionCatalogService local sha256Hex(MultipartFile)). At commit 5c5c99b2, SalesReturnService builds return keys via IdempotencySignatureBuilder + IdempotencyUtils, ProductionCatalogService uses IdempotencyUtils.sha256Hex(file.getBytes()), and commit-level grep finds no remaining private sha256Hex/normalizeToken/normalizeAmount declarations outside core.idempotency.
